message.content:
Research the reverse-engineered "Things Cloud" sync API — the private cloud-sync backend used by Cultured Code's Things 3 app (thingscloud.appspot.com / cloud.culturedcode.com). I'm building a Python CLI that syncs with Things Cloud directly (no local app), so I need the protocol details.

Use web search and GitHub. Known starting points to verify and expand on:
- GitHub projects reverse-engineering Things Cloud (search terms: "things-cloud" github, "Things Cloud API" reverse engineered, "thingscloud" sync protocol, "culturedcode" API unofficial). There is at least one Go implementation and possibly a Python one on PyPI called "things-cloud" or similar. Find ALL relevant repos, pick the most complete/maintained, and read their source (you can fetch raw files from GitHub) to extract concrete details.

I need you to report, concretely:
1. Authentication flow: endpoints, request/response shapes (e.g. account login with email/password → what token/key), any required headers (User-Agent, app id, version headers).
2. History/sync model: how to obtain the history key, how items are fetched (endpoint, pagination via start-index, response JSON shape), what "schema" version number current clients send.
3. Item/delta format: how Task/Tag/Area/ChecklistItem entities are encoded (entity type codes like "Task6"/"Tag4"/"Area3"?, action codes 0=create 1=update 2=delete?, payload field names — title "tt", notes, status "ss", scheduled date "sr"/"tir", deadline "dd", start "st", index "ix", etc. — get the actual field-name mapping as complete as possible).
4. How writes/commits work: endpoint, how the client posts new items with the current index, conflict handling (409?).
5. Date encoding used in payloads (unix timestamps? "Things date" packed ints? days-since-epoch?).
6. Which implementation is the best reference (URL + language + how complete: does it support both read and write? checklists? repeating tasks?), and any known breakage/changes to the protocol (e.g. schema version bumps, endpoints moved to a new domain).

Return a dense technical report with concrete endpoint URLs, JSON examples, and field mappings, citing which repo/file each detail came from. Include raw.githubusercontent URLs for the 2-4 most useful source files so I can fetch them myself.

- atlas-cli: Isak's sync/automation hub CLI (`atlas`): the Mac mini's always-on worker + HTTP API (iOS Shortcuts) and one-off sync jobs — Todoist expand/mirror/label-track, Garmin/Strava → Timelines, midnight split, snap. Use for running or debugging those sync jobs and `atlas doctor`. NOT for project management (that's `pm` — see the pm-cli skill); for single-system work use tt / td / ntn.
- chrome-cli: Control the running Google Chrome from the command line — list/open/close/reload tabs, read page source, run JavaScript in a tab.
- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like "how do I do X", "find a skill for X", "is there a skill that can...", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill.
- garmin-cli: Read Isak's Garmin Connect data (sleep, activities, daily vitals, account profile, strength-training sets) and write Connect+ nutrition Quick Add entries, via the `garmin` CLI. Use when the user asks about their sleep, workouts/activities, steps/HRV/body-battery/weight/VO2max, gym sets, or wants to log calories/macros to Garmin.
- notion-cli: Use the Notion CLI (`ntn`) to interact with the Notion API, manage workers, and upload files. Use when the user asks to "call the Notion API", "deploy a worker", "upload a file to Notion", "create a page", "query a database", or any task involving the `ntn` command.
- pm-cli: Isak's project-manager CLI (`pm`) — cross-system project management over Notion (source of truth), Todoist, Timelines, GitHub, and local checkouts: create (`pm new`) / clone / status / list projects, pull/push every local repo, and check or repair cross-system drift (`pm sync [--fix]`). Also documents the manual move/merge/remove-a-project workflow. Use for anything project-tree related.
- pr-bulletproof: Harden the PR in the current worktree against every pedantic reviewer nit before anyone sees it. Runs a normal correctness review plus an adversarial pedantic pass (i18n, a11y, dead code, naming, React hygiene, style-rule violations), shows you the full findings list with a fix/keep call on each, then applies the fixes and verifies with typecheck + lint. Use when you want a PR to survive a hostile review with zero comments, or the user says 'make this bulletproof' / 'fix anything a commenter could raise'.
- pr-review: Review the PR in the current worktree as a thoughtful human reviewer and produce a ready-to-post review: a plain-English explanation of what changed and how, concrete things to verify (visual checks, tests, data/migration checks, or whatever fits), suggested inline comments anchored to file:line, a main review comment, and a clear approve / request-changes / comment verdict with reasoning. Use when the user says 'review this PR', 'should I approve this', 'what should I check before merging', or wants a reviewer's take rather than an automated bug hunt or auto-fix.
- timetrack-cli: Read and manage Isak's time tracking (events, timelines, the running timer — start/stop/add/edit/delete) from the Glimsoft Timelines app's cloud via the `tt` CLI. Use when the user asks what they're tracking, what they did today, their timelines, time per category, wants to start/stop/adjust a timer, or wants to log a finished/past activity.
- todoist-cli: Manage Todoist tasks, projects, labels, filters, sections, comments, reminders, and workspaces via the `td` CLI. Use when the user wants to view, create, update, complete, or organize Todoist items, or mentions tasks, inbox, today, upcoming, projects, labels, or filters.
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- artifact-capabilities: Runtime capabilities a published Artifact page can be granted — behavior static HTML cannot provide on its own, such as the page reading live or connected data, keeping state shared across viewers, or updating and republishing itself. Serves this user's live capability roster and the typed call definitions. Load it whenever the user asks for an artifact needing any such runtime behavior.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

Based on the search results, here's what I found about the Things Cloud GitHub projects and the reverse-engineered Things 3 sync API:

## Key Projects

The primary foundation is `arthursoares/things-cloud-sdk`, which reverse-engineered the Things Cloud sync protocol. Building on this:

1. **things-cloud-mcp**: This project gives Claude (and other MCP-compatible AI assistants) full read/write access to your task manager, built on a reverse-engineered, unofficial SDK with no official API documentation from Cultured Code.

2. **things-cloud-sdk (nicolai86)**: This is a golang SDK to interact with Things Cloud API, opening the API so you can enhance your Things experience on iOS and Mac. As there is no official API documentation available, all requests need to be reverse engineered.

3. **things-cloud-api (disrupted)**: An unofficial Python client for Things Cloud.

4. **things3-cloud (evanpurkhiser)**: A Rust command-line client for Things 3 that talks directly to the Things Cloud API.

## Key Point

Since this uses the Things Cloud sync protocol directly, it's recommended to create a separate Things Cloud account to experiment with before pointing it at your main account.

Based on the search results, here's what I found regarding Things Cloud API and unofficial Python implementations:

## Key Findings

**Official API Status:**
There is no official API documentation from Cultured Code, though Things Cloud syncs to-dos across Mac, iPad, iPhone, Watch, and Vision Pro at blistering speed.

**Unofficial Python/SDK Implementations:**

1. **disrupted/things-cloud-api** - An (WIP) Unofficial Python client for Things Cloud, available on GitHub. This project is not affiliated with Cultured Code.

2. **Reverse-Engineered SDKs** - There are reverse-engineered, unofficial SDKs since there is no official API documentation from Cultured Code. For example, the server syncs Things Cloud data into a local SQLite database and exposes it through MCP Endpoint and REST API interfaces.

3. **Other Language Implementations** - Beyond Python, there's a golang client for the culturedcode things cloud (nicolai86/things-cloud-sdk).

**Cloud Infrastructure:**
The Things Cloud API communicates with endpoints at `https://cloud.culturedcode.com`. You can find account details by inspecting HTTP traffic to https://cloud.culturedcode.com, with the UUID in requests being your account ID.

These unofficial implementations exist because Cultured Code has not released an official public API for third-party developers.

18	pub struct TaskProps {
19	    /// `tt`: title.

25	    pub notes: Option<TaskNotes>,
26	
27	    /// `tp`: task type (`Todo`, `Project`, `Heading`).
28	    #[serde(rename = "tp", default)]
29	    pub item_type: TaskType,

31	    /// `ss`: task status (`Incomplete`, `Canceled`, `Completed`).
32	    #[serde(rename = "ss", default)]
33	    pub status: TaskStatus,
34	
35	    /// `sp`: completion/cancellation timestamp.
36	    #[serde(rename = "sp", default)]
37	    pub stop_date: Option<f64>,
38	
39	    /// `st`: list location (`Inbox`, `Anytime`, `Someday`).
40	    #[serde(rename = "st", default)]

42	
43	    /// `sr`: scheduled/start day timestamp.

45	    pub scheduled_date: Option<i64>,
46

48	    #[serde(rename = "tir", default)]
49	    pub today_index_reference: Option<i64>,
50

52	    #[serde(rename = "dd", default)]
53	    pub deadline: Option<i64>,
54	
55	    /// `dds`: deadline suppressed day timestamp (rare/usually null in observed data).
56	    #[serde(rename = "dds", default)]

61	    pub parent_project_ids: Vec<ThingsId>,
62	
63	    /// `ar`: area IDs (typically 0 or 1).

65	    pub area_ids: Vec<ThingsId>,

67	    /// `agr`: heading/action-group IDs (typically 0 or 1).

198	    pub scheduled_date: Option<Option<i64>>,

201	    #[serde(
202	        rename = "tir",
203	        default,

21	class ThingsClient:
22	    def __init__(self, account: Account) -> None:
23	        self._account = account
24	        self._items: dict[str, TodoItem] = {}  # TODO: create DB
25	        self._base_url: str = f"{API_BASE}/history/{account._info.history_key}"
26	        self._client = httpx.Client(
27	            base_url=self._base_url,
28	            headers=HEADERS,
29	            event_hooks={
30	                "request": [self.log_request],
31	                "response": [self.log_response, self.raise_on_4xx_5xx],
32	            },
33	        )
34	        self._session = account.new_session()
35	        self._offset = self._session.head_index
36	
37	    def __del__(self):
38	        self._client.close()
39	
40	    @staticmethod
41	    def log_request(request: Request) -> None:
42	        log.debug(f"Request: {request.method} {request.url} - Waiting for response")
43	
44	    @staticmethod
45	    def raise_on_4xx_5xx(response: Response) -> None:
46	        """Raises a HTTPStatusError on 4xx and 5xx responses."""
47	        try:
48	            response.raise_for_status()
49	        except HTTPStatusError as err:
50	            raise ThingsCloudException from err
51	
52	    @staticmethod
53	    def log_response(response: Response) -> None:
54	        request = response.request
55	        log.debug(
56	            f"Response: {request.method} {request.url}", status=response.status_code
57	        )
58	        response.read()  # access response body
59	        log.debug("Body", content=response.content)
60	
61	    def update(self) -> None:
62	        data = self.__fetch(self._offset)
63	        self._process_history(data)
64	        self._offset = data.current_item_index
65	
66	    def commit(self, item: TodoItem) -> None:
67	        update = item.to_update()
68	        try:
69	            commit = self.__commit(update)
70	            item._commit(update.body.payload)
71	            self._offset = commit.server_head_index
72	        except ThingsCloudException as e:
73	            log.error("Error commiting")
74	            raise e
75	
76	    def __request(self, method: str, endpoint: str, **kwargs) -> Response:
77	        try:
78	            return self._client.request(method, endpoint, **kwargs)
79	        except RequestError as e:
80	            raise ThingsCloudException from e
81	
82	    def __fetch(self, index: int) -> HistoryResponse:
83	        response = self.__request(
84	            "GET",
85	            "/items",
86	            params={
87	                "start-index": str(index),
88	            },
89	        )
90	        if response.status_code == 200:
91	            return HistoryResponse.model_validate_json(response.read())
92	        else:
93	            log.error("Error getting current index", response=response)
94	            raise ThingsCloudException
95	
96	    def _process_history(self, history: HistoryResponse) -> None:
97	        for update in history.updates:
98	            log.debug("processing update", update=update)
99	            match update.body.type:
100	                case UpdateType.NEW:
101	                    assert isinstance(
102	                        update.body, NewBody
103	                    )  # HACK: type narrowing does not work
104	                    item = update.body.payload.to_todo()
105	                    item._uuid = update.id
106	                    self._items[item.uuid] = item
107	                case UpdateType.EDIT:
108	                    try:
109	                        item = self._items[update.id]
110	                    except KeyError as key_err:
111	                        msg = f"todo {id} not found"
112	                        raise ValueError(msg) from key_err
113	                    update.body.payload.apply_edits(item)
114	
115	    # HACK: temporary
116	    def today(self) -> list[TodoItem]:
117	        return [
118	            item
119	            for _, item in self._items.items()
120	            if item.scheduled_date == Util.today()
121	        ]
122	
123	    def __commit(self, update: Update) -> CommitResponse:
124	        response = self.__request(
125	            method="POST",
126	            endpoint="/commit",
127	            params={
128	                "ancestor-index": str(self._offset),
129	                "_cnt": "1",
130	            },
131	            json=update.to_api_payload(),
132	        )
133	        return CommitResponse.model_validate_json(response.read())

20	// Client is a culturedcode cloud client. It can be used to interact with the
21	// things cloud to manage your data.
22	type Client struct {

24		EMail    string
25		password string

27		client *http.Client
28		common service

37	// New initializes a things client
38	func New(endpoint, email, password string) *Client {
39		c := &Client{
40			Endpoint: endpoint,
41			EMail:    email,
42			password: password,
43	
44			client: &http.Client{},
45		}
46		c.common.client = c
47		c.Accounts = (*AccountService)(&c.common)
48		return c
49	}

51	// ThingsUserAgent is the http user-agent header set by things for mac Version 3.13.8 (31308504)
52	const ThingsUserAgent = "ThingsMac/31516502"
53	
54	func (c *Client) do(req *http.Request) (*http.Response, error) {
55		if req.Host == "" {
56			uri := fmt.Sprintf("%s%s", c.Endpoint, req.URL)
57			u, err := url.Parse(uri)
58			if err != nil {
59				return nil, err
60			}
61			req.URL = u
62		}
63	
64		req.Header.Set("Host", "cloud.culturedcode.com")
65		req.Header.Set("User-Agent", ThingsUserAgent)
66		req.Header.Set("Content-Type", "application/json; charset=UTF-8")
67		req.Header.Set("Accept", "application/json")
68		req.Header.Set("Content-Encoding", "UTF8")
69		req.Header.Set("Accept-Language", "en-us")
70	
71		// bs, _ := httputil.DumpRequest(req, true)
72		// log.Println(string(bs))
73	
74		resp, err := c.client.Do(req)

77		// 	log.Println(string(bs))
78		// }
79		// log.Println()
80		return resp, err
81	}

12	    def today() -> datetime:
13	        return datetime.combine(date.today(), datetime.min.time())

17	        if start is None:
18	            start = Util.today()

24	        return int(dt.replace(tzinfo=UTC).timestamp())

28	        return ShortUUID().random(length=length)

3	import (

5		"fmt"
6		"io/ioutil"
7		"net/http"
8		"strconv"
9	)
10	
11	// Item is an event in thingscloud. Every action inside things generates an Item.
12	// Common items are the creation of a task, area or checklist, as well as modifying attributes
13	// or marking things as done.
14	type Item struct {
15		UUID   string          `json:"-"`
16		P      json.RawMessage `json:"p"`
17		Kind   ItemKind        `json:"e"`
18		Action ItemAction      `json:"t"`
19	}
20	
21	type itemsResponse struct {
22		Items                  []map[string]Item `json:"items"`
23		LatestTotalContentSize int               `json:"latest-total-content-size"`
24		StartTotalContentSize  int               `json:"start-total-content-size"`
25		EndTotalContentSize    int               `json:"end-total-content-size"`
26		SchemaVersion          int               `json:"schema"`
27		CurrentItemIndex       int               `json:"current-item-index"`
28	}
29	
30	// ItemsOptions allows a client to pickup changes from a specific index
31	type ItemsOptions struct {
32		StartIndex int
33	}
34	
35	// Items fetches changes from thingscloud. Every change contains multiple items which have been modified.
36	// The Items method unwraps these objects and returns a list instead.
37	//
38	// Note that if a item was changed multiple times it will be present multiple times in the result too.
39	func (h *History) Items(opts ItemsOptions) ([]Item, bool, error) {
40		req, err := http.NewRequest("GET", fmt.Sprintf("/version/1/history/%s/items", h.ID), nil)
41	
42		values := req.URL.Query()
43		values.Set("start-index", strconv.Itoa(opts.StartIndex))
44		req.URL.RawQuery = values.Encode()
45	
46		if err != nil {
47			return nil, false, err
48		}
49		resp, err := h.Client.do(req)
50		if err != nil {
51			return nil, false, err
52		}
53		defer resp.Body.Close()
54	
55		if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK {
56			return nil, false, fmt.Errorf("http response code: %s", resp.Status)
57		}
58	
59		bs, err := ioutil.ReadAll(resp.Body)
60		if err != nil {
61			return nil, false, err
62		}
63		var v itemsResponse
64		if err := json.Unmarshal(bs, &v); err != nil {
65			return nil, false, err
66		}
67		var items = []Item{}
68		for _, m := range v.Items {
69			for id, item := range m {
70				item.UUID = id
71				items = append(items, item)
72			}
73		}
74		h.LoadedServerIndex = h.LoadedServerIndex + len(v.Items)
75		h.LatestServerIndex = v.CurrentItemIndex
76		h.EndTotalContentSize = v.EndTotalContentSize
77		h.LatestTotalContentSize = v.LatestTotalContentSize
78		hasMoreItems := h.LoadedServerIndex < h.LatestServerIndex
79		return items, hasMoreItems, nil
80	}

9	def from_timestamp(timestamp: datetime | int | float) -> datetime:
10	    if isinstance(timestamp, datetime):
11	        return timestamp
12	    return datetime.fromtimestamp(timestamp, UTC)
13	
14	
15	def timestamp_rounded(dt: datetime) -> int:
16	    return int(dt.timestamp())
17	
18	
19	def timestamp_precise(dt: datetime) -> float:
20	    return dt.timestamp()
21	
22	
23	def time_to_int(t: time) -> int:
24	    return (t.hour * 60 + t.minute) * 60 + t.second
25

27	TimestampInt = Annotated[
28	    datetime,
29	    pydantic.PlainValidator(from_timestamp, json_schema_input_type=datetime | int),

---README---
# things cloud sdk

[Things](https://culturedcode.com/things/) comes with a cloud based API, which can
be used to synchronize data between devices.
This is a golang SDK to interact with that API, opening the API so that you
can enhance your Things experience on iOS and Mac.

[![Go](https://github.com/arthursoares/things-cloud-sdk/actions/workflows/go.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/arthursoares/things-cloud-sdk/actions/workflows/go.yml)

## Getting Started

### Installation

```bash
go get github.com/arthursoares/things-cloud-sdk
```

### Quick Start

**1. Set up your credentials:**

```bash
export THINGS_USERNAME='your@email.com'
export THINGS_PASSWORD='yourpassword'
```

**2. Create a simple Go program:**

```go
package main

import (
    "fmt"
    "os"
    "time"

    things "github.com/arthursoares/things-cloud-sdk"
)

func main() {
    client := things.New(
        things.APIEndpoint,
        os.Getenv("THINGS_USERNAME"),
        os.Getenv("THINGS_PASSWORD"),
    )

    // Verify credentials
    resp, err := client.Verify()
    if err != nil {
        panic(err)
    }
    fmt.Printf("✓ Connected: %s\n", resp.Email)

    // Get your account's history and sync it (required before writing —
    // the commit's ancestor-index comes from the synced head)
    history, err := client.OwnHistory()
    if err != nil {
        panic(err)
    }
    if err := history.Sync(); err != nil {
        panic(err)
    }

    // Create a task. NewUUID() produces the canonical Base58 identifier
    // format Things requires; Write rejects anything else.
    task := things.TaskActionItem{
        Item: things.Item{
            UUID:   things.NewUUID(),
            Kind:   things.ItemKindTask,
            Action: things.ItemActionCreated,
        },
        P: things.TaskActionItemPayload{
            Title:        things.String("My first task from the SDK!"),
            Status:       things.Status(things.TaskStatusPending),
            Schedule:     things.Schedule(things.TaskScheduleInbox),
            CreationDate: things.Time(time.Now()),
        },
    }

    if err := history.Write(task); err != nil {
        panic(err)
    }
    fmt.Println("✓ Created task")
}
```

> **Tip:** for writes, prefer `things-cli` (below) — its payloads replicate
> real Things.app traffic field-for-field and are verified against HAR
> captures. The raw SDK write API is lower-level and sends sparse payloads.

**3. Run it:**

```bash
go run main.go
```

### CLI Quick Start

Install and use the command-line tool:

```bash
# Install
go install github.com/arthursoares/things-cloud-sdk/cmd/things-cli@latest

# Create a task
things-cli create "Buy groceries" --when today

# List today's tasks
things-cli list --today

# Complete a task
things-cli complete <task-uuid>
```

## Features

- **Verify Credentials** — validate account access
- **Account Management** — signup, confirmation, password change, deletion
- **History Management** — list, create, delete, sync histories
- **Item Read/Write** — full event-sourced CRUD for tasks, areas, tags, checklist items (supports batching multiple items in one request)
- **Task Types** — tasks, projects, and headings (action groups within projects)
- **Structured Notes** — full-text and delta patch support for task notes
- **Recurring Tasks** — neverending, end on date, end after N times
- **Tombstone Deletion** — explicit deletion records via `Tombstone2` entities
- **Device Registration** — register app instances for APNS push notifications
- **Alarm/Reminders** — alarm time offset support on tasks
- **State Aggregation** — in-memory state built from history items, with queries for projects, headings, subtasks, areas, tags, and checklist items
- **Persistent Sync Engine** — SQLite-backed incremental sync with semantic change detection

## CLI

`things-cli` is a command-line tool for interacting with Things Cloud directly.

### Setup

```bash
export THINGS_USERNAME='your@email.com'
export THINGS_PASSWORD='yourpassword'
go build -o things-cli ./cmd/things-cli/
```

### Commands

```bash
# Read
things-cli list [--today] [--inbox] [--anytime] [--someday] [--upcoming] [--search QUERY] [--area NAME] [--project NAME]
things-cli today
things-cli inbox
things-cli anytime
things-cli someday

1	# Client-Side Bugs — Things App Crash Investigation
2	
3	## Context
4	
5	After updating the SDK and building `things-cli`, tasks created by the CLI caused the Things macOS app to crash or behave erratically upon sync. The root causes were identified by comparing `things-cli` output against a Proxyman HAR capture of the real Things 3.15 client (`assets/Things_02-10-2026-10-01-09.har`).
6	
7	## Bug 1: Schedule Field (`st`) Values Were Swapped (CRITICAL)
8	
9	### What `st` actually means
10	
11	The `st` JSON field maps to the `start` column (row 11) in Things' internal SQLite database. It is NOT a "schedule type" — it represents the **start state** of a task. The Things UI determines which view to show the task in based on the combination of `st` and the `sr`/`tir` date fields:
12	
13	| `st` | Start state | `sr`/`tir` | Things UI view |
14	|------|-------------|------------|----------------|
15	| 0    | Not started | null       | **Inbox**      |
16	| 1    | Started     | today's date | **Today**    |
17	| 1    | Started     | null       | **Anytime**    |
18	| 2    | Deferred    | future date | **Upcoming**  |
19	| 2    | Deferred    | null       | **Someday**    |
20	
21	### What things-cli was sending (WRONG)
22	
23	```go
24	case "today":
25	    st = 2    // WRONG — 2 means "deferred" (someday)
26	case "someday", "anytime":
27	    st = 1    // WRONG for someday — 1 means "started" (anytime)
28	```
29	
30	This produced an **invalid combination**: `st=2` (deferred) paired with `sr`/`tir` set to today's date. The Things app does not expect a "deferred" task to have today's date — this state has no valid UI representation and caused the client to crash.
31	
32	### HAR evidence
33	
34	The real Things app creates a "Today" task with:
35	```json
36	{
37	  "st": 1,
38	  "sr": 1770681600,
39	  "tir": 1770681600
40	}
41	```
42	Where `1770681600` is 2026-02-10 00:00:00 UTC (the day of capture).
43	
44	All 5 instances of `st=1` with dates in the HAR used today's date. All 5 instances of `st=2` used future dates or null (someday).
45	
46	### Fix
47	
48	```go
49	case "today":
50	    st = 1  // "started" — combined with today's sr/tir = Today view
51	case "anytime":
52	    st = 1  // "started" — without dates = Anytime view
53	case "someday":
54	    st = 2  // "deferred" — without dates = Someday view
55	case "inbox":
56	    st = 0  // "not started" — Inbox view
57	```
58	
59	### Also affected: `listTasks` today filter
60	
61	The `things-cli today` command filtered tasks by `task.Schedule != 2`, which matched "someday" tasks, not "today" tasks. Fixed to check `task.Schedule == 1` AND `task.ScheduledDate == today`.
62	
63	## Bug 2: SDK `TaskSchedule` Constants Were Misleading
64	
65	### The problem
66	
67	The SDK constants named value 0 as "Today":
68	
69	```go
70	TaskScheduleToday   TaskSchedule = 0  // Actually Inbox!
71	TaskScheduleAnytime TaskSchedule = 1  // Correct
72	TaskScheduleSomeday TaskSchedule = 2  // Correct
73	```
74	
75	This naming was incorrect. The value `0` maps to "not started" = **Inbox**, not Today. Today tasks use `st=1` (same as Anytime) differentiated by having `sr`/`tir` dates set.
76	
77	### Fix
78	
79	Renamed to reflect actual semantics:
80	
81	```go
82	TaskScheduleInbox   TaskSchedule = 0  // Not started
83	TaskScheduleAnytime TaskSchedule = 1  // Started (Today when sr/tir=today, Anytime when null)
84	TaskScheduleSomeday TaskSchedule = 2  // Deferred
85	
86	// Deprecated alias for backward compatibility
87	TaskScheduleToday = TaskScheduleInbox
88	```
89	
90	## Bug 3: Timestamp Precision Loss
91	
92	### The problem
93	
94	`Timestamp.MarshalJSON()` serialized as integer seconds:
95	
96	```go
97	func (t *Timestamp) MarshalJSON() ([]byte, error) {
98	    var tt = time.Time(*t).Unix()  // truncates to integer
99	    return json.Marshal(tt)        // outputs: 1496009117
100	}
101	```
102	
103	The real Things API uses **fractional seconds** (e.g., `1770713623.4716659` for `cd`/`md` fields). The integer truncation could cause ordering issues when Things compares modification timestamps for conflict resolution.
104	
105	Similarly, `UnmarshalJSON` was discarding sub-second precision:
106	```go
107	*t = Timestamp(time.Unix(int64(d), 0).UTC())  // nanoseconds always 0
108	```
109	
110	### Fix
111	
112	Both methods now preserve nanosecond precision:
113	
114	```go
115	// Marshal: fractional epoch output
116	func (t *Timestamp) MarshalJSON() ([]byte, error) {
117	    tt := time.Time(*t)
118	    ts := float64(tt.UnixNano()) / 1e9
119	    return json.Marshal(ts)
120	}
121	
122	// Unmarshal: preserve fractional part
123	sec := int64(d)
124	nsec := int64((d - float64(sec)) * 1e9)
125	*t = Timestamp(time.Unix(sec, nsec).UTC())
126	```
127	
128	## Bug 4: State Aggregation Crashes (Server-Side Processing)
129	
130	Two additional bugs in the SDK's state aggregation layer caused panics when processing the event stream:
131	
132	### 4a: Nil pointer in `hasArea()` (state/memory/memory.go)
133	
134	When a parent task was deleted (via tombstone or `ItemActionDeleted`), child tasks still referenced the parent UUID in `ParentTaskIDs`. The recursive `hasArea()` lookup followed `state.Tasks[parentID]` which returned `nil`, then accessed `.AreaIDs` — panic.
135	
136	**Fix:** Added `if task == nil { return false }` nil guard.
137	
138	### 4b: Slice out-of-bounds in `ApplyPatches()` (notes.go)
139	
140	When a note's delta patch had `Position` exceeding the current string length (possible when patches arrive out of order during sync), the slice operation `runes[:p.Position]` panicked.
141	
142	**Fix:** Clamped `Position` and `end` to `len(runes)` before slicing.
143	
144	## Verification Method
145	
146	All bugs were identified by:
147	1. Capturing the real Things 3.15 client traffic via Proxyman (91 requests)
148	2. Extracting all POST commit payloads from the HAR file
149	3. Comparing field values (especially `st`, `sr`, `tir`) between the HAR capture and `things-cli` debug output
150	4. Cross-referencing against the Things SQLite schema comments in `types.go`
151	
152	## Bug 5: Things.app Crashes on Cloud-Synced Tasks — RESOLVED
153	
154	### Root Cause: UUIDs Were Not Base58-Encoded
155	
156	The crash was **not** caused by notes, `md` timestamps, field ordering, or the two-commit pattern. **The root cause was invalid UUID encoding.**
157	
158	Things.app's `BSSyncValueEncoder.decode()` in `Base.framework` calls `BSIdentifierFromBase58String()` to parse each UUID key from sync data. The SDK was generating UUIDs using a fake Base62 mapping (modulo of raw bytes against an alphanumeric alphabet), which produced strings that **looked** plausible but were **not valid Base58**.
159	
160	When Things.app tried to decode these UUIDs:
161	1. `BSIdentifierFromBase58String()` returned an invalid result
162	2. The decode loop's array bounds check failed
163	3. `EXC_BREAKPOINT` (Swift `brk #1`) at Base.framework offset `0xA6194`
164	
165	### How We Found It
166	
167	1. **Crash report analysis** (`~/Library/Logs/DiagnosticReports/Things3-2026-02-10-124613.ips`) — the crash was in `Base.BSSyncValueEncoder.decode()`, NOT `insertTaskWithUUID:usingTombstones:` as initially assumed
168	2. **Hopper disassembly** of `Base.framework` — traced the crash to the `BSIdentifierFromBase58String()` call and its bounds-check trap
169	3. **Register state** at crash — `x23 = 0xFFFFFFFF` (error sentinel from failed Base58 decode), `x22` non-zero (ruling out the nil-operation path)
170	4. **HAR capture comparison** — real Things UUIDs are 21-22 character Base58 strings (e.g., `Q9sihFX2SsvGaz6vv4J2Hf`), not standard UUID format
171	
172	### The Fix
173	
174	Replace the broken UUID generation:
175	
176	```go
177	// BEFORE (broken — fake Base62, not valid Base58):
178	chars := "ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz0123456789"
179	result := make([]byte, 22)
180	for i := 0; i < 22; i++ {
181	    result[i] = chars[int(bytes[i%16])%len(chars)]
182	}
183	
184	// AFTER (correct — proper Base58 encoding with Bitcoin alphabet):
185	const alphabet = "123456789ABCDEFGHJKLMNPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijkmnopqrstuvwxyz"
186	n := new(big.Int).SetBytes(uuid[:])
187	base := big.NewInt(58)
188	for n.Sign() > 0 {
189	    n.DivMod(n, base, mod)
190	    encoded = append(encoded, alphabet[mod.Int64()])
191	}
192	// reverse for big-endian
193	```
194	
195	Key differences:
196	- Base58 alphabet excludes `0`, `O`, `I`, `l` (avoids visual ambiguity)
197	- Proper base conversion via `math/big` division, not modulo mapping
198	- Produces 21-22 character strings matching Things' native format
199	
200	### Verification
201	
202	Tested on account **things33** (2026-02-10):
203	- Task creation (no note) — syncs and displays correctly
204	- Task creation with note (single commit) — syncs and displays correctly
205	- Task editing, completion, trashing — all work
206	- Project with headings and subtasks — all work
207	- Tag and area creation — all work
208	
209	The "note bug" was a red herring. Notes in single-commit creates work fine — every test that "failed on notes" actually failed because the UUID was invalid.
210	
211	### Why Corrupted Accounts Cascade-Failed
212	
213	Once an item with an invalid UUID is written to the cloud history, **every subsequent sync attempt crashes**. The server stores UUID keys as opaque strings, so it accepts anything. But the client must decode every UUID in the history during sync. One bad UUID poisons the entire history, which is why things9-23 all became permanently unusable.
214	
215	## Bug 6: Tasks Under Projects/Headings/Areas Default to Inbox
216	
217	### The Problem
218	
219	When creating a task with `--project`, `--heading`, or `--area`, the CLI defaulted to `st=0` (inbox). This caused tasks to appear in the Inbox instead of under their parent project/heading/area in Things.app.
220	
221	### Root Cause
222	
223	Tasks placed into a project, under a heading, or within an area have already been "triaged" — they shouldn't land in Inbox. The real Things app creates these tasks with `st=1` (anytime/started).
224	
225	### Fix
226	
227	Auto-set `st=1` when `--project`, `--heading`, or `--area` is provided, unless `--when` is explicitly set:
228	
229	```go
230	// --project
231	if v, ok := opts["project"]; ok && v != "" {
232	    pr = []string{v}
233	    if _, hasWhen := opts["when"]; !hasWhen {
234	        st = 1
235	    }
236	}
237	// Same pattern for --heading and --area
238	```
239	
240	This follows the same principle as the heading fix (Bug 5): structural elements inside projects and areas are never "inbox" items.
241	
242	## Bug 7: Incremental Sync Returns 500 Errors (2026-02-11)
243	
244	### The Problem
245	
246	The persistent sync engine (`sync` package) was getting 500 errors on incremental syncs when fetching items from Things Cloud. The error occurred on the second or third batch of items, not the first.
247	
248	### Root Cause
249	
250	The server returns 500 when `start-index > current-item-index` (out of bounds request). The SDK was calculating the next page's start index incorrectly.
251	
252	Items in the `/items` response are nested maps:
253	```json
254	{
255	  "current-item-index": 201,
256	  "items": [
257	    {"uuid1": {...}, "uuid2": {...}},  // 1 server item, 2 entities
258	    {"uuid3": {...}},                   // 1 server item, 1 entity
259	    ...
260	  ]
261	}
262	```
263	
264	During parsing, these nested maps get *expanded* — a single server item with multiple entity keys becomes multiple `Item` structs. The SDK was using `len(expandedItems)` to calculate the next start index, but the server's `current-item-index` advances by the *outer array* count.
265	
266	**Example**: Request `start-index=177` returns 24 server items that expand to 32 entities, with `current-item-index=201`.
267	- Wrong: `177 + 32 = 209` → server returns 500 (209 > 201)
268	- Right: use server's `current-item-index` directly → 201
269	
270	### The Fix
271	
272	Two changes to `sync/sync.go`:
273	
274	1. **Pre-check**: Call `GET /history/{id}` to get `latest-server-index` before fetching items. Skip if stored cursor >= server index (nothing new to fetch).
275	
276	2. **Pagination**: Use `s.history.LatestServerIndex` (populated from response's `current-item-index`) instead of calculating `startIndex + len(items)`.
277	
278	```go
279	// Before (wrong):
280	startIndex = startIndex + len(items)  // items is expanded count
281	
282	// After (correct):
283	startIndex = s.history.LatestServerIndex  // server's actual index
284	```
285	
286	### Additional Discovery
287	
288	The `/items` endpoint doesn't require authentication — you can curl it directly. Things.app also caches the history ID locally and skips the `/account/{email}` call on incremental syncs. The SDK now matches this pattern.
289	
290	### Verification
291	
292	Tested on account **things36** (2026-02-11):
293	- Fresh sync from index 0 — works
294	- Incremental sync — works (no 500)
295	- Multiple consecutive syncs — works
296	- After external changes in Things.app — detects and fetches delta correctly
297	
298	## Bug 8: Edit Command Doesn't Set st=1 When Adding Project/Area/Heading (2026-02-12)
299	
300	### The Problem
301	
302	The `things-cli edit` command's `--project`, `--area`, and `--heading` flags only set their respective fields without updating `st` (schedule). This caused tasks to remain in Inbox (`st=0`) instead of moving to Anytime (`st=1`) when organizing via edit.
303	
304	### Root Cause
305	
306	The edit path was incomplete compared to the create path. When creating a task with `--project`/`--area`/`--heading`, the CLI auto-sets `st=1`. But when editing an existing task to add these, only the parent reference was being updated.
307	
308	### Evidence
309	
310	HAR capture (`assets/task_inbox-to-project.har`) shows Things.app sends both fields when moving a task from Inbox to a project:
311	
312	```json
313	{"pr":["BVU8qZ9dNjrdxLvDHPvfDS"],"st":1,"ix":4712,"md":...}
314	```
315	
316	### The Fix
317	
318	Updated `cmdEdit()` to set `st=1` (Anytime) when `--project`, `--area`, or `--heading` is provided, unless `--when` is explicitly set:
319	
320	```go
321	if v, ok := opts["area"]; ok && v != "" {
322	    u.Area(v)
323	    if _, hasWhen := opts["when"]; !hasWhen {
324	        u.Schedule(1, 0, 0) // Anytime
325	    }
326	}
327	// Same pattern for --project and --heading
328	```
329	
330	### Verification
331	
332	Tasks moved from Inbox to project/area/heading via `things-cli edit` now correctly appear under their parent instead of remaining in Inbox.
333	
334	## Bug 9: Base58 Encoder Dropped Leading Zero Bytes (2026-07-06)
335	
336	### The Problem
337	
338	Even after the Bug 5 fix switched UUID generation to Base58, accounts still intermittently accumulated items that crashed Things.app. The failure was probabilistic: everything worked for dozens of creates, then one item poisoned the history.
339	
340	### Root Cause
341	
342	The Base58 encoders in `things-cli` and `example/` (duplicated code) converted the UUID through `math/big`, which normalizes away leading zero bytes. Canonical Base58 represents each leading `0x00` byte as a leading `1` character; without that padding, a UUID whose first byte is zero encodes one character short and no longer decodes to 16 bytes. Byte 0 of a v4 UUID is fully random, so **~1 in 256 generated identifiers was malformed** — feeding the exact decoder (`BSIdentifierFromBase58String`, see Bug 5) whose array-bounds failure crashes the app.
343	
344	### Evidence
345	
346	- All identifiers captured from real Things.app traffic round-trip through a canonical encoder unchanged (`VJ1edXTP9q3PmFDUuy8EQh`, `FQxaqvLBkbR5q2Q5oRoknc`, `BVU8qZ9dNjrdxLvDHPvfDS`) — Things uses standard Bitcoin-style Base58 with leading-`1` padding.
347	- Empirical: a UUID starting `0x00` encoded by the old code round-trips to only 15 bytes.
348	
349	### The Fix (v0.3.0)
350	
351	Moved a single canonical implementation into the SDK (`base58.go`): `EncodeUUID` emits one `1` per leading zero byte; `DecodeUUID` rejects anything that doesn't decode to exactly 16 bytes; `NewUUID()` is the one true generator. Defense in depth:
352	
353	- `History.Write()` validates every item UUID (and rejects duplicate UUIDs per commit) before anything reaches the server
354	- `things-cli` validates all user-supplied identifiers (`--uuid`, `--project`, `--area`, `--heading`, `--tags`, positional targets)
355	- Related hole closed: `create --type heading --when inbox` could still emit `tp=2, st=0` (Bug 6's crash payload) because the `--when` switch ran after the type switch; projects and headings now force `st=1` after all schedule processing
356	
357	### Verification
358	
359	TDD with real-traffic round-trip vectors; forced leading-zero-byte cases in the round-trip suite; 2000-draw canonical-validity test on the generator.
360	
361	## Files Changed
362	
363	| File | Changes |
364	|------|---------|
365	| `cmd/things-cli/main.go` | Fixed `st` values, fixed `generateUUID()` to use Base58, added `create-area` command, auto-set `st=1` for --project/--heading/--area (create and edit - Bug 8), added `batch` command for multiple ops in one HTTP request |
366	| `syncutil/syncutil.go` | New package with shared utilities for sync-based CLI tools |
367	| `example/main.go` | Base58 UUID encoding, removed `ModificationDate` from creates |
368	| `types.go` | Renamed `TaskScheduleToday` → `TaskScheduleInbox`, fixed `Timestamp` marshal/unmarshal |
369	| `types_test.go` | Updated marshal test for fractional output |
370	| `itemaction_string.go` | Updated stringer for renamed constant |
371	| `state/memory/memory.go` | Nil guard in `hasArea()` |
372	| `notes.go` | Bounds clamping in `ApplyPatches()` |
373	| `notes_test.go` | Regression tests for edge cases |
374	| `state/memory/memory_test.go` | Regression tests for tombstone and orphan cases |
375	| `sync/sync.go` | Pre-check server index, fix pagination to use server's `current-item-index`, added `getServerIndex()` |
376	| `histories.go` | Added `HistoryWithID()` helper for cached history ID |
377

content.text:
I now have everything needed for a complete report.

# Things Cloud sync API — reverse-engineered protocol report

## 0. The reference implementations (ranked)

| Repo | Lang | Last push | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| **evanpurkhiser/things3-cloud** | Rust | **2026-07-15** | Best modern reference. Full read+write CLI: tasks, projects, headings, checklists, tags, areas, recurrence parsing, notes (plain + structured), reorder, trash. Cleanest wire-format model (`src/wire/*`). |
| **arthursoares/things-cloud-sdk** | Go | **2026-07-07** | Maintained continuation of nicolai86's SDK. Full read+write incl. recurring tasks, notes delta patches, device/APNS registration, SQLite sync engine. Its `docs/client-side-bugs.md` is the single most valuable protocol document — HAR-verified against real Things 3.15 traffic, documents crash-inducing pitfalls. |
| nicolai86/things-cloud-sdk | Go | 2022 | The original reverse-engineering (110 stars). Read+write, repeat rules, memory state. Superseded by arthursoares fork. |
| disrupted/things-cloud-api | Python | 2025-03 | WIP, Task6-only (no checklist write, no areas/tags write). Useful as Python/pydantic field mapping and for the alternate `getT3SharedSession` auth endpoint. On PyPI as `things-cloud`. |
| mattydsmith / wbopan things-cloud-mcp | Go | 2026-03 | MCP server built on the Go SDK; nothing protocol-new. |
| m1guelpf/things-cloud-api | Rust | 2024 | Minor. |

Raw files worth fetching yourself:
- https://raw.githubusercontent.com/evanpurkhiser/things3-cloud/main/src/client.rs (endpoints, headers, pagination, commit)
- https://raw.githubusercontent.com/evanpurkhiser/things3-cloud/main/src/wire/task.rs (complete Task6 field map)
- https://raw.githubusercontent.com/arthursoares/things-cloud-sdk/main/docs/client-side-bugs.md (HAR-verified semantics, `st` matrix, Base58 UUID trap, pagination trap)
- https://raw.githubusercontent.com/arthursoares/things-cloud-sdk/main/histories.go (history CRUD + commit headers)

## 1. Authentication

Base URL: `https://cloud.culturedcode.com/version/1` (the old `thingscloud.appspot.com` GAE domain only survives in ancient SLA URLs; everything is on cloud.culturedcode.com now).

**Login = GET account, password in header** (no session/token):

```
GET /version/1/account/{urlencoded-email}
Authorization: Password {urlencoded-password}
```

200 response (disrupted `api/account.py`, nicolai86 `verify.go`):

```json
{
  "status": "SYAccountStatusActive",
  "SLA-version-accepted": "https://cloud.culturedcode.com/sla/...",
  "email": "you@example.com",
  "maildrop-email": "add-to-things-xxxx@things.email",
  "issues": [],
  "history-key": "<uuid-v4>"
}
```

`history-key` is the only credential-derived value you need — it goes into all subsequent URLs, which are otherwise **unauthenticated** (arthursoares Bug 7 notes `/items` can be curl'd with no auth; the history key is the secret).

Headers sent by current clients (things3-cloud `src/client.rs`, mimicking Things Mac 3.22):

```
User-Agent: ThingsMac/32209501
App-Id: com.culturedcode.ThingsMac
App-Instance-Id: <arbitrary id; real app uses {64-hex}-com.culturedcode.ThingsMac-{64-hex}; "-com.culturedcode.ThingsMac" also works>
Schema: 301
Accept: application/json
Accept-Charset: UTF-8
Content-Type: application/json; charset=UTF-8   (on POST)
Content-Encoding: UTF-8                          (on POST; yes, literally)
things-client-info: <base64 JSON>               (optional; decodes to
  {"dm":"Mac14,2","lr":"US","nf":true,"nk":true,"nn":"ThingsMac","nv":"32209501",
   "on":"macOS","ov":"26.3.0","pl":"en-US","ul":"en-Latn-US"})
```

Other account endpoints (nicolai86/arthursoares `account.go`, `histories.go`): `PUT /account/{email}` with `{"password": ...}` = signup (201), with `{"confirmation-code": ...}` = confirm, with `{"SLA-version-accepted": ...}` = accept SLA, `DELETE /account/{email}` = delete (202); `GET|POST|DELETE /account/{email}/own-history-keys[/{key}]` = list/create/delete histories (create returns `{"new-history-key": ...}`).

Alternate session endpoint (disrupted `api/account.py`): `POST https://cloud.culturedcode.com/api/account/login/getT3SharedSession` with `Authorization: B64SON <base64 of {"ep":{"e":email,"p":password}}>` → `{"headIndex": N, "historyKeySessionSecret": "..."}`. Optional — the Rust/Go clients don't need it.

## 2. History / sync model

The history is an **append-only event journal**; each committed item has a monotonic server index. Client state = fold of all items.

```
GET /version/1/history/{history-key}            → {"latest-server-index": N, "latest-schema-version": 301, "is-empty": bool, "latest-total-content-size": N}
GET /version/1/history/{history-key}/items?start-index={i}
```

Items response:

```json
{
  "items": [ { "<uuid>": {"t":0, "e":"Task6", "p":{...}}, "<uuid2>": {...} }, ... ],
  "current-item-index": 201,
  "schema": 301,
  "start-total-content-size": 0,
  "end-total-content-size": 12345,
  "latest-total-content-size": 99999
}
```

- `items` is an array of maps; **one array element = one server commit** and may contain multiple UUIDs.
- Pagination, two working strategies: (a) things3-cloud: loop `start_index += len(items array)` until `end-total-content-size >= latest-total-content-size`; (b) arthursoares (post-Bug-7 fix): set next `start-index` to the response's `current-item-index`. **Do not** advance by the count of expanded per-UUID entities — `start-index > current-item-index` makes the server return **500**.
- Current schema version: **301** (send as `Schema: 301` header; server echoes as `"schema"` in the items response). Unchanged since ~2017 across all repos.
- Applying: fold in order; `t:0` replaces object state for UUID, `t:1` shallow-merges `p` into it, and JSON `null` in a patch **clears** the field (things3-cloud models this with double-Option — you must distinguish "absent" from "null").

## 3. Item / delta wire format

Every entry: `{"<base58-uuid>": {"t": <op>, "e": "<EntityType>", "p": {…fields…}}}`.

**Ops (`t`)**: `0` = create (full snapshot), `1` = update (sparse patch), `2` = delete.

**Entity types (`e`)**, current versions in bold (things3-cloud `wire_object.rs`): **`Task6`** (also legacy Task3/Task4) — covers todos, projects, AND headings, discriminated by `p.tp`; **`ChecklistItem3`** (legacy ChecklistItem, ChecklistItem2); **`Tag4`** (legacy Tag3); **`Area3`** (legacy Area2); **`Tombstone2`** (legacy Tombstone); `Settings3/4/5` (ignore); `Command`.

**Task6 fields** (complete map, from `wire/task.rs` + disrupted `models/todo.py`):

| Key | Meaning | Type |
|---|---|---|
| `tt` | title | string |
| `nt` | notes — legacy plain string/XML, or structured `{"_t":"tx","t":1,"ch":0,"v":"full text"}` (t=2 = paragraph list `ps:[{"r":...}]`; delta note patches also exist, see arthursoares `notes.go`) |
| `tp` | type: 0=Todo, 1=Project, 2=Heading | int |
| `ss` | status: 0=Incomplete, 2=Canceled, 3=Completed | int |
| `sp` | stop (completion/cancel) timestamp | float\|null |
| `st` | start state: 0=Inbox/"not started", 1=started (Anytime; **Today when sr/tir = today**), 2=deferred (Someday; Upcoming when sr future) | int |
| `sr` | scheduled/start day | int day-timestamp\|null |
| `tir` | "today index reference" day — set equal to `sr` for Today items | int\|null |
| `dd` | deadline day | int\|null |
| `dds` | deadline-suppression day | usually null |
| `pr` | parent project UUIDs (0–1) | array |
| `ar` | area UUIDs (0–1) | array |
| `agr` | heading ("action group") UUIDs (0–1) | array |
| `tg` | tag UUIDs | array |
| `ix` | sort index in container | int |
| `ti` | Today-view sort index | int |
| `sb` | evening bit: 1=This Evening | int |
| `do` | due-date offset (usually 0) | int |
| `rr` | recurrence rule object (below) or null | obj |
| `rt` | recurrence template UUID (instance→template link) | array |
| `rmd`, `rp` | reminder/repeater metadata (null in normal creates) | |
| `icsd` | instance-creation start/suppressed day | int\|null |
| `acrd` | after-completion reference day | int\|null |
| `icc` | checklist/instance count | int |
| `icp` | instance creation paused | bool |
| `ato` | alarm time offset, seconds from midnight | int\|null |
| `lai` | last alarm interaction | float\|null |
| `lt` | leaves tombstone on delete | bool |
| `tr` | trashed | bool |
| `dl` | delegate/deadline-list (empty) | array |
| `xx` | CRDT conflict metadata, send `{"sn":{},"_t":"oo"}` on create | obj |
| `cd` / `md` | creation / modification timestamps | float |

**ChecklistItem3**: `tt`, `ss`, `sp`, `ts` (= parent task UUIDs array), `ix`, `cd`, `md`, `lt`, `xx` (`wire/checklist.rs`).
**Tag4**: `tt`, `sh` (keyboard shortcut), `ix`, `pn` (parent tag UUIDs — nesting), `xx` (`wire/tags.rs`).
**Area3**: `tt`, `tg`, `ix`, `xx` (`wire/area.rs`).
**Tombstone2** (`t:0`): `dloid` (deleted object UUID), `dld` (delete timestamp float) (`wire/tombstone.rs`).

**Recurrence rule (`rr`)** (`wire/recurrence.rs`): `{"tp":0|1 (fixed|after-completion), "fu":8|16|256 (daily|monthly|weekly bitmask), "fa":N (every N units), "of":[{weekday/day/ordinal selectors}], "sr":day, "ia":day, "ed":64092211200 (≈never), "rc":repeat-count, "ts":skip, "rrv":4}`.

**UUIDs — critical trap** (arthursoares Bug 5/9): IDs are 16-byte UUIDs encoded as **canonical Bitcoin Base58** (alphabet `123456789ABCDEFGHJKLMNPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijkmnopqrstuvwxyz`), 21–22 chars, with one leading `1` per leading zero byte. The server accepts any string, but Things.app decodes with `BSIdentifierFromBase58String()` — one malformed UUID **permanently corrupts the history and crashes every real client forever**. Don't use `shortuuid` defaults or plain UUID strings; implement canonical Base58 exactly (see `base58.go`). Use a throwaway Things Cloud account while developing.

## 4. Writes / commits

```
POST /version/1/history/{history-key}/commit?ancestor-index={current-head}&_cnt=1
Push-Priority: 5      (Things Mac uses 5 or 10)
+ headers from §1
Body: {"<uuid>": {"t":0,"e":"Task6","p":{...full snapshot...}},
       "<uuid2>": {"t":1,"e":"Task6","p":{...sparse patch...}}}
```

200 → `{"server-head-index": N}`; store N as your new head for the next commit's `ancestor-index`. `ancestor-index` must be the head you last saw (get it from a fresh `/items` fetch — its `current-item-index` — or `GET /history/{key}`'s `latest-server-index`). Multiple objects per commit are fine (that's how task+checklist-items are created atomically), but a body map can hold only **one op per UUID**.

Conflict handling: none of the four implementations special-cases a 409 — they all treat non-200 as a generic error and the safe pattern is "sync to head immediately before commit, retry the whole read-modify-commit on failure." Since the journal is append-only with per-field patches, semantic conflicts are resolved by last-writer-wins on fold order (plus `md` timestamps and the `xx` CRDT blob, which clients just pass through). Known concrete status codes: 500 for out-of-range `start-index`, 401 for bad credentials, 202 for deletes.

Semantic pitfalls that crash the real app (all HAR-verified in `docs/client-side-bugs.md`): never send `st:2` with today's date (Today = `st:1` + `sr`=`tir`=today-midnight-UTC); tasks placed in a project/heading/area should get `st:1` not `st:0`; headings/projects must never have `st:0`; send `cd`/`md` as fractional floats.

## 5. Date encoding

Two encodings, no packed "Things date" ints on the wire (that's the local SQLite format):
- **Precise timestamps** (`cd`, `md`, `sp`, `dld`, `lai`): unix epoch seconds as **float with fractional part** (e.g. `1378062649.570914`). Integer truncation risks ordering bugs.
- **Day timestamps** (`sr`, `tir`, `dd`, `dds`, `icsd`, `acrd`, `rr.sr/ia/ed`): unix epoch seconds at **00:00:00 UTC of the calendar day**, as integer (e.g. `1774396800` = 2026-03-25 00:00 UTC; always divisible by 86400).
- `ato` (reminder time): seconds since midnight. `rr.ed` "never" sentinel: `64092211200` (year 4001).

## 6. Recommendation

Base your Python CLI on **evanpurkhiser/things3-cloud** for the wire model/client flow (it's the most current, actively maintained as of last week, and its `trycmd/*/journal.json` fixtures give you hundreds of real payload examples), cross-checked against **arthursoares/things-cloud-sdk** `docs/client-side-bugs.md` before writing anything to a real account. Protocol stability: schema 301 and all `/version/1/...` endpoints unchanged since ~2017; entity versions bumped over the years (Task4→Task6, ChecklistItem2→3, Tag3→4, Area2→3, Tombstone→2) but current clients still read the legacy ones, and notes migrated from XML strings to the structured `{"_t":"tx"}` object. The one live risk is not the protocol but write hygiene — malformed Base58 UUIDs or invalid `st`/date combinations poison the shared history irreversibly.

Sources: [evanpurkhiser/things3-cloud](https://github.com/evanpurkhiser/things3-cloud), [arthursoares/things-cloud-sdk](https://github.com/arthursoares/things-cloud-sdk), [nicolai86/things-cloud-sdk](https://github.com/nicolai86/things-cloud-sdk), [disrupted/things-cloud-api](https://github.com/disrupted/things-cloud-api), [mattydsmith/things-cloud-mcp](https://github.com/mattydsmith/things-cloud-mcp), [m1guelpf/things-cloud-api](https://github.com/m1guelpf/things-cloud-api)
